---
title: "Multivariate functional principal component eigenfunctions of the entero-pancreatic system: conceptual and physiological foundations of the FDEP-TP framework for periprandial phenotype characterisation"
subtitle: "Lancet Diabetes & Endocrinology — Article (Original Research)"
author:
  - name: "Héctor M. Virgen-Ayala"
    email: hectorvirgenmd@gmail.com
    orcid: 0009-0006-2081-2286
    affiliations:
      - "Department of Surgical Clinics, Universidad de Guadalajara, Guadalajara, Mexico"
      - "Department of General Surgery, Hospital General de Zona No. 14, Instituto Mexicano del Seguro Social, Guadalajara, Mexico"
date: "2026-05-09"
format:
  docx:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=2.2cm
    fontsize: 11pt
    mainfont: "Georgia"
    monofont: "Menlo"
    linkcolor: blue
    citecolor: blue
keep-tex: false
bibliography: refs_A1.bib
csl: ../lancet.csl
---

<!--
Article A1 — conceptual / physiological layer (L1) of the FDEP-TP trilogy.
This file is a SKELETON committed at the start of the A1 branch (Day 8 per
meta-prompt §10·2).  Companion paper A2 (covariance operators, BLUP, Golovkine)
is on branch `lancet-A2-mathematical` (tag `A2-submitted-2026-05-09`).
Companion paper A3 (PTP/IEP procedural-translational) is on branch
`lancet-A3-methodological`.

Each TODO marks prose to be drafted on Days 8–14.
-->

# Summary

<!-- TODO Day 9 — 300-word structured Summary in Lancet style:
Background / Methods / Findings / Interpretation / Funding.
Anchor figures from numerical_ledger_A1.md (✶ shared across A1/A2/A3):
N_studies=23 · N_arms=55 · N_pseudo=2,750 · K=12 · FVE 92% · N/K 29.2 ·
PC1 incretin loading 0·211 · PYY total dominance 0·762.
Emphasis: physiological interpretation of the four leading mFPC eigenfunctions
of the joint enteropancreatic operator. -->

# Research in context

**Evidence before this study.** <!-- TODO Day 10 — search terms covering
incretin physiology, GLP-1 / GIP / PYY / ghrelin postprandial dynamics,
enteropancreatic axis review (Drucker 2018, Holst 2007, Steinert 2017),
ghrelin suppression and Y-receptor satiety physiology (le Roux 2006,
Madsbad 2014), Lancet D&E precedent on mechanism reviews. -->

**Added value of this study.** <!-- TODO Day 10 — first physiological
interpretation of the four leading mFPC eigenfunctions of the joint operator,
mapped to: incretin–satiety contrast (PC1 PYY-dominant with secondary GLP-1),
proximal-vs-distal entero-endocrine sequencing (PC2), biphasic glucose–insulin
coupling (PC3), and ghrelin tone (PC4). -->

**Implications of all the available evidence.** <!-- TODO Day 12 — clinical
implications restricted to physiological interpretation; defer therapeutic
decision tree to companion paper A3. -->

# Introduction

<!-- TODO Day 8 — open with the physiological gap, NOT epidemiology (anti-F8):
the entero-pancreatic axis is a coupled secretion↔inhibition oscillator on
the periprandial transition; existing taxonomies decompose static variables
and lose this coupled geometry; the four conceptual moves of the FDEP-TP
framework are stated as the operational construct of this article:
(i) periprandial transition as the unit of analysis;
(ii) physiologic periprandial responsiveness (replacing "secretory capacity");
(iii) weight as a contextual modifier (Laferrère 2008; Rubino 2025);
(iv) non-obese without T2D as the unique reference distribution. -->

# Methods

## Study design and data sources

<!-- TODO Day 8 — short paragraph: ecological proof-of-concept at the
cohort-time-arm level; 23 studies, 71 tuples, 55 arms, 2,750 pseudo-subjects;
master_table.csv SHA-256 2829cd78018e411783671ec00f849647858bda552cfa4ec23ad505ba9704a117;
OSF DOI 10.17605/OSF.IO/3CZRE.  REUSE the boilerplate from A2 §Methods §Study
design with prose envelope rewritten (iThenticate-prevention). -->

## Periprandial sampling and analyte panel

<!-- TODO Day 8 — same source as A2 with envelope rewritten; emphasis on the
physiological coverage rather than the design-analysis quantities. -->

## Conceptual framework

<!-- TODO Day 9 — narrative version of the four conceptual moves:
periprandial transition unit; physiologic periprandial responsiveness;
weight as modifier; non-obese reference distribution.  Each move framed
in physiological terms with classical citations (Drucker, Holst, Steinert,
Le Roux, Madsbad, Nauck). -->

## Statistical estimation (referenced to A2)

> The covariance operator construction, BLUP estimation, Golovkine 2025
> component selection, and bootstrap stability machinery are the subject of
> companion paper A2 (Lancet Diabetes & Endocrinology, branch
> `lancet-A2-mathematical`, tag `A2-submitted-2026-05-09`).  Briefly: nine
> analyte forms entered the joint Happ–Greven operator with Chiou weights;
> K=12 components were retained at the multivariate-FVE ≥ 0·90 threshold;
> n=350 was the smallest joint block; bootstrap stability was ≥ 80 %.

## Software, reproducibility, and reporting

<!-- TODO Day 13 — boilerplate identical structure to A2 with prose envelope
rewritten; emphasise that the same canonical run feeds A1, A2, A3. -->

## Role of the funding source

There was no funding for this study. The author had full access to all data and final responsibility for the decision to submit for publication.

# Findings

## Cohort and panel composition

<!-- TODO Day 9 — Table 1 cohort composition; same anti-F8 figures as A2. -->

## Four leading mFPC eigenfunctions: physiological interpretation

### Eigenfunction 1 — distal L-cell dominance

<!-- TODO Day 9 — interpret PC1 in terms of PYY total dominance (‖ψ̂_1^{(PYY tot)}‖² = 0·762)
and the integrated incretin loading (0·211); link to distal L-cell
density gradient and post-bariatric anatomical exposure. -->

### Eigenfunction 2 — proximal-vs-distal sequencing

<!-- TODO Day 10 — interpret PC2 as early GIP / late GLP-1, PYY contrast;
proximal K-cell vs distal L-cell sequencing along the small bowel. -->

### Eigenfunction 3 — biphasic glucose–insulin coupling

<!-- TODO Day 11 — interpret PC3 as biphasic insulin response coupled to
glucose absorption kinetics; reference Polonsky 1988 incretin classics and
the modern reinterpretation in Drucker 2018. -->

### Eigenfunction 4 — ghrelin tone

<!-- TODO Day 11 — interpret PC4 as fasting ghrelin tone vs postprandial
suppression; link to fundic resection signature in SG (le Roux 2006, Svane 2019). -->

## Cohort-level signatures (qualitative)

<!-- TODO Day 12 — qualitative cohort-level signatures of the four
eigenfunctions across the 7 cohorts; defer quantitative IEP Type prevalences
to A3. -->

# Discussion

## Principal physiological findings

<!-- TODO Day 12 — recap of the four-eigenfunction interpretation; integrative
narrative: secretion↔inhibition oscillator visible at the joint-operator level. -->

## Comparison with existing physiological taxonomies

<!-- TODO Day 13 — Ahlqvist 2018, Udler 2018, Tübingen, German Diabetes Study;
glucose-curve FDA precedents (Frøslie 2013, Hulman 2018); Glucotypes (Hall 2018);
PREDICT (Berry 2020). -->

## Mechanistic interpretation

<!-- TODO Day 13 — bariatric L-cell exposure mechanism; ghrelin suppression by
fundic resection; caloric restriction physiology; pharmacological extension to
dual / triple receptor agonists (Frías 2021, Heise 2022, Rosenstock 2023). -->

## Strengths and limitations

<!-- TODO Day 13 — physiological strengths; six declared limitations as in
companion papers; defer methodological strengths to A2 and clinical
limitations to A3. -->

## Implications for research and practice

<!-- TODO Day 13 — physiological implications; defer therapeutic algorithm to
companion paper A3. -->

## Conclusion

<!-- TODO Day 14 — three-sentence physiological synthesis. -->

# Contributors

HMVA conceived the conceptual framework, derived the physiological interpretation of the four leading mFPC eigenfunctions, performed the empirical evaluation, drafted the manuscript, and approved the final version.

# Declaration of interests

The author declares no competing interests, no industry support, and no funding for this work.

# Data sharing

All digitised periprandial trajectories, the harmonised cohort corpus (`master_table.csv`, SHA-256 `2829cd78018e411783671ec00f849647858bda552cfa4ec23ad505ba9704a117`), the 16-script analytic pipeline, the article-specific outputs, and the reproducibility scripts are deposited at Zenodo (concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19743544; article-specific version DOIs cited in each manuscript) and GitHub (`sv8wmxnbp8-hash/EPP10` v1·3 and subsequent branches) under CC-BY 4·0. The repository permits full reproduction of every numerical claim and follows FAIR data principles. Pre-registration is at the Open Science Framework (DOI 10.17605/OSF.IO/3CZRE; project at https://osf.io/tr469, frozen 2026-04-22).

# AI/LLM disclosure

Analytic pipeline design, R code implementation, and manuscript drafting were supported by Claude (Anthropic, Opus 4.7 / Sonnet 4.6, May 2026). All statistical outputs, pre-registration decisions, and scientific claims were validated by the author against the v10·0 master analysis plan (OSF DOI 10.17605/OSF.IO/3CZRE) and the FDEP-TP framework v2·0 reference document.

# Acknowledgments

<!-- TODO Day 14 — physiological acknowledgments; classical incretin literature
authors. -->

---

# References

::: {#refs}
:::

---

# Display item captions

**DI1 — Architecture of the Hilbert product space H = ∏_j L²(T_j) with Chiou weights.** <!-- TODO Day 10 caption. -->

**DI2 — Four leading mFPC eigenfunctions with physiological annotation.** <!-- TODO Day 10 caption. -->

**DI3 — Cohort-level mean trajectories with simultaneous bootstrap bands.** <!-- TODO Day 11 caption. -->

**DI4 — Heatmap of PTP per-analyte labels by cohort.** <!-- TODO Day 11 caption. -->

---

# Tables

## Table 1 — Cohort, panel, and reference adequacy

<!-- TODO Day 9 — same anti-F8 cohort composition table as A2 Table 1. -->

## Table 2 — Eigenfunction loadings by analyte and component

<!-- TODO Day 10 — A1-specific table (not in A2): integrated squared L²-norm
‖ψ̂_m^{(j)}‖² for m=1..4, j=1..9 hormone forms.  Highlights PC1 PYY 0.762,
incretin loading 0.211, etc. -->

## Table 3 — Reference physiological signatures (literature-anchored)

<!-- TODO Day 11 — A1-specific table cross-referencing each eigenfunction
to landmark physiological references (Drucker, Holst, Steinert, Madsbad,
Nauck). -->
